title: California lawmaker confirms the stateâ€™s Senate will not take up E15 bill
content:
The California Senate will not take up E15 legislation unanimously passed by the California Assembly earlier this month during the legislatureâ€™s special session to prevent gas price spikes. The bill aimed to accelerate the stateâ€™s adoption of E15. California Gov. Gavin Newsom on Aug. 31 called for a special session of the California Legislature to address the problem of gasoline price spikes. One of the bills introduced during the special session aimed to expedite required work that is currently underway by state agencies to allow E15 sales within the state. California is the only state that has not already taken action to allow E15. 
California Assemblywoman Cottie Petrie-Norris on Sept. 24 introduced the E15 bill, ABX2-9. The legislation on Sept. 27 unanimously passed out of the Petroleum and Gasoline Supply Committee. The California Assembly on Oct. 1 voted 68 to zero in favor of the bill.
author: Analysts Account
